title: N-ACETYL SELANK
description: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC) is a derivative of the amino acid L-cysteine that serves as a precursor to glutathione, the body's primary antioxidant. It works by replenishing glutathione levels, supporting liver detoxification, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating glutamate neurotransmission. NAC is FDA-approved as a medication for acetaminophen overdose and is commonly used as a supplement for respiratory health, mental health conditions, and overall antioxidant support.

research.title: N-Acetylcysteine Ineffective in Alleviating Hangover from Binge Drinking: A Clinical Study
research.summary: A clinical trial found that NAC did not significantly reduce hangover symptoms from binge drinking, challenging its popular use for alcohol-related recovery.

research.title: The use of N-acetylcysteine in the prevention of hangover: a randomized trial
research.summary: A randomized controlled trial tested NAC for hangover prevention, finding limited effectiveness in reducing alcohol-induced symptoms.

research.title: N acetylcysteine in the treatment of alcohol use disorder: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
research.summary: A double-blind RCT examined NAC's efficacy in treating alcohol use disorder, with results showing potential benefits for reducing alcohol consumption and cravings.

sentiment.summary: Users report NAC as highly effective for anxiety, OCD, compulsive behaviors, autoimmune conditions, and respiratory health. While some experience no noticeable effects and a few report negative reactions like insomnia or brain fog, the majority find it beneficial for mental health, inflammation reduction, and immune support, with many taking it long-term without issues.

effects.label: Onset Time:
effects.description: Effects typically appear within 2 hours to 2 weeks, with mental health benefits often taking longer to fully manifest

effects.label: Consistency:
effects.description: Works well for some users with no tolerance buildup over years of use, while others report no noticeable effects even after extended trials

effects.label: Synergy:
effects.description: Often more effective when combined with glycine (as GlyNAC) for glutathione synthesis, sleep quality, and recovery benefits

effects.label: Individual Response:
effects.description: Highly variable effectiveness, with genetic factors and specific conditions influencing outcomes significantly

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: 600mg once or twice daily is the most commonly reported dosage, typically taken in the morning or split throughout the day

dosage.label: Therapeutic Range:
dosage.description: Higher doses of 1000-1800mg daily are used for specific conditions like autoimmune disease, OCD, or intensive recovery protocols

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Often taken with food to reduce potential stomach upset, though some take it on an empty stomach for better absorption

dosage.label: Cycling:
dosage.description: Most users take it daily long-term without cycling, though some recommend occasional breaks to assess continued need

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Issues:
sideEffects.description: Nausea and stomach discomfort are the most commonly reported side effects, especially at higher doses or on empty stomach

sideEffects.label: Sleep Disruption:
sideEffects.description: Some users report insomnia, restlessness, or overstimulation when taken too late in the day or at high doses

sideEffects.label: Rare Negative Reactions:
sideEffects.description: A small subset reports brain fog, headaches, or worsening anxiety, though these are uncommon and may indicate dosing issues

sideEffects.label: Generally Well-Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: Most users report no side effects with standard dosing, and long-term use appears safe for the majority

availability.label: FDA Controversy:
availability.description: NAC was approved as a drug in 1963, leading to FDA attempts to remove it from the supplement market, though it remains widely available

availability.label: Common Sources:
availability.description: Available from major supplement retailers, though online platforms have restricted sales due to regulatory concerns

availability.label: Cost:
availability.description: Relatively inexpensive supplement, with bulk powder forms offering significantly better value than capsules
